data.four_set_effect: While protected by a shield, gain an additional 40% Normal and Charged Attack DMG.

circlet_of_logos.desc: A popular mask cast in the image of a deity, as described in the legends.
circlet_of_logos.title: Summer Night's Mask

flower_of_life.desc: A man-made flower in eternal bloom. Who knows if there truly is life in there?
flower_of_life.title: Summer Night's Bloom

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: Water balloons can be seen everywhere during the summer festival, but none are as finely-wrought as this one.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Summer Night's Waterballoon

plume_of_death.desc: A well-crafted wooden dart. It will only stop once it has reached its destination.
plume_of_death.title: Summer Night's Finale

sands_of_eon.desc: A pocketwatch that has stopped at a certain point in time.
sands_of_eon.title: Summer Night's Moment

list.desc: A summer festival flower that blooms forever,</br>That will not wilt even if buried deep below the snow.That will not wilt even if buried deep below the snow.</br>Some may label it an imitation, an false life,</br>For life lies in change, pain and growth,</br>In meetings, and also in partings.</br>But the memories of meeting her,</br>Of watching the fireworks bloom in the sky like fresh flowers together,</br>The memories of that foxy-eyed woman who eventually disappeared without a trace...</br>That unwithering flower is the final thing to remember her by.</br>In the end, the difference comes down to the fact that for some,</br>Life is as eternal as this undying summer bloom,</br>But for most, it is as transient as smoke.</br>
list.title: Summer Night's Bloom

list.desc: An intricately-made throwing dart. It is a common sight during the summer festival.</br>In the ghost stories of Inazuma,</br>There is a tale about a meeting between the human and inhuman...</br>To celebrate my wife's pregnancy, I went to the shrine to give a votive offering.</br>But for reasons unknown, I went up the mountain with these objects.</br>The water balloon from when I seven, the fox mask from when I was seventeen,</br>And a flower that would not wilt in ten or even a hundred years.</br>Why did I expect to meet her again?</br>No matchmaker introduced myself and my wife, and we were always short on money,</br>And it took some time for us to produce an heir,</br>But our days were still filled with happiness, were they not?</br>But still I detoured on that mountain road to the place where I'd seen the fireworks with her.</br>Pulling the bushes apart, I thought I saw her dressed in white, sitting upon that rock.</br>But when I came forward and looked, it was just a fox sunbathing.</br>It leaped up at the sound of snapping branches, and fled into the woods,</br>And like the spots of light that poke between leaves moved by the breeze, it was gone in a flash.</br>All that was left was an old wooden throwing dart.</br>
list.title: Summer Night's Finale

list.desc: A small, ornate pocket watch.</br>The watch seems to have stopped at a certain time of day.</br>In the ghost stories of Inazuma,</br>This has something to do with an encounter with something inhuman...</br>On the night of the summer festival, as I walked the path to the shrine with the girl I admired,</br>I heard, only barely, the sound of a lost child's cries.</br>In that moment of distraction, I fell, spraining my ankle and breaking that pocket watch.</br>While she ran to look for some ointment,</br>I tried to make way for the people passing through,</br>And sat down on a rock by the wayside to rest.</br>The beautiful mask-wearing woman sat down beside me.</br>"There are few who know about this spot."</br>"But it's the best place to watch the fireworks, you know?"</br>It should have been a dream.</br>We hadn't met in ten years,</br>And she had aged not a single day in those years, and yet...</br>"You've grown so much. Looks like we should pass on the game of fusen."</br>"I've brought wine, though. Let's watch the fireworks together. What do you say?"</br>
list.title: Summer Night's Moment

list.desc: A beautiful water balloon filled with water.</br>In the ghost stories of Inazuma,</br>This is a memento from a chance meeting with something inhuman...</br>During the summer festival, I was separated from my parents.</br>It was but a moment, but I had wanted to look at the water balloons,</br>And let go my hand that had gripped on to my father's sleeve.</br>Before we knew it, the tide of people escorting the divine palanquin had washed us apart.</br>I cried by the torii gates along the road to the shrine,</br>And I counted the feet of the people ascending the mountain.</br>I do not know when it happened,</br>But a beautiful lady with fox-like eyes had come to my side, and taken my hand.</br>"How preposterous, to leave such an adorable child here."</br>"So, how about it? Would you like to see the fireworks, throw some darts, and play fusen with me?"</br>
list.title: Summer Night's Waterballoon

list.desc: The mask of one indwelt by a deity.</br>A mask that has been cast in the image of some legendary deity.</br>People will often take on the guise of the fox of legend,</br>Covering their faces with masks based on her divine visage,</br>Perhaps wishing that they might gain her ability of transformation.</br>In Inazuma's legends, everything has a spirit.</br>...But even so,</br>It is likely that most such beings would have long fled,</br>Driven into the forests by the suppression of the Shogun.</br>But many people still believe in these divine foxes and the ability to be indwelt by them...</br>They believe that thousands of years of age may confer power upon animals.</br>As such, they also believe in what this fox mask represents.</br>A note has been left on the back of this mask, written in a lovely hand.</br>"I'm sorry, I departed under the cover of the fireworks."</br>"We will most likely never meet again. Take care of yourself."</br>
list.title: Summer Night's Mask
